problem:
The matrix
\[\begin{pmatrix} \frac{4}{29} & -\frac{10}{29} \\ -\frac{10}{29} & \frac{25}{29} \end{pmatrix}\]corresponds to projecting onto a certain vector $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix}.$  Find $\frac{y}{x}.$

classified_questions.question: What is the matrix describing and what vector is it projecting onto?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How does projecting a vector onto itself help us find the value of y/x?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we use other matrices to project onto the same vector, and if so, how would their corresponding values of y/x compare to the one we found?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the matrix multiplication involved in the projection relate to solving the system of equations?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What other applications does projecting a vector onto another vector have in mathematics and physics?
classified_questions.label: e
